description: Detect and track sessions from Codex CLI, Copilot CLI, Cursor, and Windsurf alongside existing Claude Code tracking

userStories.title: AiToolDetector trait: shared abstraction for all AI tool detectors
userStories.description: Define a `AiToolDetector` trait in a new `src/ai_tracking.rs` module. All per-tool detectors implement this trait. The trait drives poll_all_ai_sessions() in poller.rs, replacing the direct call to poll_claude_sessions(). The trait must be object-safe so detectors can be stored as `Vec<Box<dyn AiToolDetector>>`.
acceptanceCriteria: Trait defined in src/ai_tracking.rs: `pub trait AiToolDetector { fn tool_name(&self) -> &'static str; fn poll(&self, conn: &Connection, watched_repos: &[PathBuf]); }`
Module registered as `pub mod ai_tracking;` in src/lib.rs
Existing `ClaudeDetector` struct in ai_tracking.rs wraps the existing poll_claude_sessions_with_paths logic (delegates to it)
ClaudeDetector implements AiToolDetector
`pub fn poll_all_ai_sessions(conn: &Connection, watched_repos: &[PathBuf])` in ai_tracking.rs: builds Vec<Box<dyn AiToolDetector>> with all detectors, calls poll() on each
poller.rs full_scan() replaces `claude_tracking::poll_claude_sessions(conn, &repos)` with `ai_tracking::poll_all_ai_sessions(conn, &repos)`
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB: tool field populated on insert_ai_session
userStories.description: Modify `db::insert_ai_session` to accept a `tool: &str` parameter and write it to the `tool` column (currently hardcoded to DEFAULT 'claude-code'). Update all callers. The schema already has the column — no migration needed.
acceptanceCriteria: `insert_ai_session(conn, tool, repo_path, session_id, started_at)` — tool param added as second arg
SQL: `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ai_sessions (tool, repo_path, session_id, started_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)`
ClaudeDetector calls insert_ai_session with tool='claude-code'
All other callers updated (compiler catches this)
Existing tests updated to pass tool='claude-code'
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Process inspection: is_process_running by name/pattern
userStories.description: Add `fn processes_matching(name_pattern: &str) -> Vec<u32>` in ai_tracking.rs. Uses `/proc` on Linux or `sysctl` (via `sysinfo` crate or raw `ps` subprocess) on macOS to enumerate running process names. Used by Cursor, Windsurf, and Copilot detectors that have no PID file. Falls back gracefully to empty vec if enumeration fails. Keep the existing `is_process_running(pid)` in claude_tracking.rs unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: `fn processes_matching(name_pattern: &str) -> Vec<u32>` in ai_tracking.rs — returns PIDs of running processes whose name contains name_pattern (case-insensitive)
macOS: uses `pgrep -i <name_pattern>` subprocess with 2s timeout; parses stdout lines as u32 PIDs
Linux: same approach (pgrep available on both platforms)
On pgrep failure or timeout: returns empty vec (never panics)
`fn is_any_process_running(name_pattern: &str) -> bool` — convenience wrapper: processes_matching(...).is_empty() == false
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Codex CLI detector
userStories.description: Implement `CodexDetector` in ai_tracking.rs. Scans `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl`. Each file is JSONL where the first line is a `SessionMeta` object with `cwd`, `createdAt` (ISO string), `updatedAt`. Session ID derived from file path (date + filename stem). 'Still running' detection: process named `codex` is running AND file's updatedAt is within last 5 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: CodexDetector implements AiToolDetector; tool_name() returns 'codex'
Scans `~/.codex/sessions/` recursively for files matching `rollout-*.jsonl`; skips dirs that don't exist (graceful degradation)
Parses first JSONL line as `CodexSessionMeta { cwd: String, created_at: String, updated_at: String }` with #[serde(rename_all = 'camelCase')]
Session ID: `{YYYY-MM-DD}-{filename_stem}` e.g. `2024-03-15-rollout-abc123`
Calls insert_ai_session(conn, 'codex', repo_path, session_id, created_at) — INSERT OR IGNORE
Session ends: codex process not running OR updated_at older than 5 minutes → call update_session_ended with ended_at=updated_at (not now)
map_to_repo used to resolve cwd → watched repo path (same as Claude pattern)
Turns: count of non-empty lines in the JSONL file (same as Claude count_turns pattern)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Copilot CLI detector
userStories.description: Implement `CopilotDetector` in ai_tracking.rs. Scans `~/.copilot/session-state/*/workspace.yaml` files. Each directory is a session (UUID as session_id). workspace.yaml contains `cwd`. Session start time from directory mtime if not in YAML. events.jsonl in same dir provides activity count (turn equivalent).
acceptanceCriteria: CopilotDetector implements AiToolDetector; tool_name() returns 'copilot-cli'
Scans `~/.copilot/session-state/` for subdirectories; skips if path doesn't exist
For each subdir: reads workspace.yaml with serde_yaml (add as dependency if not present); extracts `cwd: String` field
Session ID: the UUID directory name
Session start time: mtime of workspace.yaml via `std::fs::metadata().modified()` → RFC3339 string; fallback to current time if unavailable
Calls insert_ai_session(conn, 'copilot-cli', repo_path, session_id, started_at)
Turn count: count non-empty lines in events.jsonl (same directory) if file exists; None otherwise
'Still running': process named `copilot` is running AND workspace.yaml mtime within last 5 minutes
Session ends: mark with ended_at = mtime of workspace.yaml
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Cursor detector
userStories.description: Implement `CursorDetector` in ai_tracking.rs. Cursor stores state in `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/globalStorage/state.vscdb` (SQLite). cwd comes from `workspaceStorage/<hash>/workspace.json` files in the same Cursor User directory. Session = one open workspace. 'Still running': process named `Cursor` is running.
acceptanceCriteria: CursorDetector implements AiToolDetector; tool_name() returns 'cursor'
macOS path: `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/workspaceStorage/`; Linux path: `~/.config/Cursor/User/workspaceStorage/`; skips if path doesn't exist
For each `<hash>/workspace.json`: parse JSON to extract `folder` field (the workspace cwd). Skip if folder is absent or not a local path (skip `vscode-remote://` URIs)
Session ID: `cursor-{hash}` where hash is the directory name
Started_at: mtime of workspace.json → RFC3339
Calls insert_ai_session(conn, 'cursor', repo_path, session_id, started_at)
'Still running': `is_any_process_running('Cursor')` is true AND workspace.json mtime within last 30 minutes
Session ends: ended_at = now (Cursor doesn't record session end time)
turns: None (no conversation log available)
Platform-gated: path selection uses `#[cfg(target_os = 'macos')]` / else for Linux
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Windsurf detector
userStories.description: Implement `WindsurfDetector` in ai_tracking.rs. Windsurf's data directory is poorly documented; detection is primarily process-based. Strategy: detect that Windsurf is running, derive cwd from active git repo inference (check watched repos for recent git activity), create a synthetic session per watched repo that had activity while Windsurf was running.
acceptanceCriteria: WindsurfDetector implements AiToolDetector; tool_name() returns 'windsurf'
macOS path attempted: `~/Library/Application Support/Windsurf/User/workspaceStorage/` — same structure as Cursor if it exists; parse workspace.json files identically
If Windsurf data dir not found: fall back to process-only detection

Process-only mode: if `is_any_process_running('Windsurf')` → create/update a synthetic session per watched_repo that had a git commit in the last 30 minutes (query git_activity table for repo_path WHERE timestamp > now-30min)
Synthetic session ID: `windsurf-{repo_slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}` where repo_slug is last path component
starts_at: start of current day (midnight UTC) for synthetic sessions
Session ends: Windsurf process not running → mark all open Windsurf sessions as ended with ended_at=now
turns: None
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB: query active sessions filtered by tool
userStories.description: Add `get_active_sessions_by_tool(conn, tool) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>>` in db.rs. Used by per-tool detectors to check only their own sessions when marking ended. Also add `get_active_sessions_all(conn) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<(String, String)>>` returning (session_id, tool) pairs — used by the generic cleanup path.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub fn get_active_sessions_by_tool(conn: &Connection, tool: &str) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<String>>` — SELECT session_id WHERE ended_at IS NULL AND tool = ?1
`pub fn get_active_sessions_all(conn: &Connection) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<(String, String)>>` — SELECT session_id, tool WHERE ended_at IS NULL
Both return empty vec if no rows (not an error)
Existing get_active_sessions() unchanged (still used by ClaudeDetector)
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Output: display tool name in ai_sessions section
userStories.description: Update output.rs pretty and JSON rendering to show the tool name for each AI session. In pretty output, prefix the session line with the tool name. In JSON, the tool field is already present in ai_sessions — ensure it's included in JsonAiSession.
acceptanceCriteria: JsonAiSession gains `pub tool: String` field
JSON output: ai_sessions array entries include `"tool": "claude-code"` (or relevant tool name)
Pretty output: session line shows tool name, e.g. `claude-code  session-id  12m  8 turns` (tool in first column)
Existing tests for output.rs updated to include tool field in JsonAiSession construction
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: DB: query to include tool in ai_sessions output
userStories.description: Update query.rs AiSession struct and query to SELECT the tool column. Currently AiSession has session_id, started_at, ended_at, duration_minutes, turns — add tool field.
acceptanceCriteria: AiSession struct gains `pub tool: String` field
SELECT query in query.rs includes `tool` column
All test constructions of AiSession updated (compiler catches)
query_activity returns RepoSummary.ai_sessions with tool populated
`cargo build` passes

userStories.title: Unit tests: AiToolDetector trait and ClaudeDetector
userStories.description: In tests/ai_tracking_test.rs: verify ClaudeDetector wraps poll_claude_sessions correctly. Test that poll_all_ai_sessions calls each detector. Use mock/stub pattern: a TestDetector that records whether poll() was called.
acceptanceCriteria: TestDetector struct implements AiToolDetector, sets a flag when poll() is called
Test: poll_all_ai_sessions with TestDetector → flag is set
Test: ClaudeDetector with empty sessions dir → no panic, no DB rows inserted
Test: ClaudeDetector with one valid session JSON → DB row inserted with tool='claude-code'
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: Codex detector parsing
userStories.description: In tests/ai_tracking_test.rs: create a temp dir mimicking ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl. Verify parsing, session ID generation, insert behavior, and ended detection.
acceptanceCriteria: Create temp sessions dir with one rollout-abc.jsonl containing valid SessionMeta JSON on line 1 + 3 more JSONL lines
After poll(): DB has one ai_sessions row with tool='codex', session_id contains date + 'rollout-abc', turns=4
Second poll() with same file: no duplicate (INSERT OR IGNORE)
File with invalid/missing first-line JSON: skipped without panic
Sessions dir doesn't exist: poll() returns without error
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: Copilot detector parsing
userStories.description: In tests/ai_tracking_test.rs: create temp dir mimicking ~/.copilot/session-state/<uuid>/workspace.yaml + events.jsonl. Verify parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Temp dir with one UUID subdir containing workspace.yaml (`cwd: /tmp/test-repo`) and events.jsonl with 5 lines
After poll(): DB has one row with tool='copilot-cli', session_id=UUID, turns=5
workspace.yaml without cwd field: session skipped
No events.jsonl: turns=None
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: Cursor detector parsing
userStories.description: In tests/ai_tracking_test.rs: create temp workspaceStorage dir with hash subdirs and workspace.json files. Verify local path parsing and vscode-remote URI skipping.
acceptanceCriteria: Temp workspaceStorage dir with one hash subdir, workspace.json containing `{"folder": "/tmp/test-repo"}`
After poll(): DB has one row with tool='cursor', session_id='cursor-{hash}'
workspace.json with `{"folder": "vscode-remote://ssh-remote+host/path"}`: row NOT inserted
workspace.json missing folder key: row NOT inserted
All tests pass

userStories.title: Unit tests: process detection helper
userStories.description: In tests/ai_tracking_test.rs: verify processes_matching and is_any_process_running behave correctly. Cannot test live process detection hermetically, so test the error-handling paths and that the function returns a Vec (not panics).
acceptanceCriteria: processes_matching('definitely-not-a-real-process-name-xyz') returns empty vec (not an error)
is_any_process_running('definitely-not-a-real-process-name-xyz') returns false
processes_matching('') returns vec (possibly non-empty, just must not panic)
All tests pass

`blackbox today --format json` output: ai_sessions array contains entries with tool='claude-code', tool='codex', tool='cursor'
All three rows present in JSON output
All tests pass
